text: Starting college next month. Budget Rs 15,000. Need 3 oversized tees, 2 cargos, 2 jeans, 1 hoodie. Black/grey only. Delhi. Hostel.
notes: Primary demo Script A, pre-clarifier. Gender must stay null here — this is what triggers the clarifying question, never a guess.

text: Men's college wardrobe. Budget Rs 15,000. Need 3 oversized tees, 2 cargos, 2 jeans, 1 hoodie. Black/grey only.
notes: Script A after the user answers the gender clarifier explicitly.

text: Wedding to attend next month, want to look good, around 5k
notes: Script B. No literal garment word, so item extraction is out of scope for this text (occasion-based synthesis happens downstream of the parser, after gender is clarified).

text: 5 outfits under Rs 3,000
notes: Script C — honesty-under-impossible-budget. "outfits" is not a garment word, so raw item extraction is empty by design; the shortfall has to be handled downstream, not invented here.

text: Mera bhai ke liye 2 kurta aur 1 jeans chahiye, budget 4000 rupees, black colour mein
knownLimitation: "Mera bhai ke liye" (Hindi for "for my brother") does not match the English-only relational-phrase regex, so gender stays unknown even though a human reader would say Men. The system's fallback here is to ask, which is safe, but it's a real code-mixed-language gap worth closing before a Hindi-heavy demo.
notes: Budget and items extract correctly despite the code-mixed sentence — only the relational gender phrase is missed.

text: Meri behen ki shaadi hai, lehenga chahiye, budget around 20000
notes: Contrast case to hinglish_brother_kurta: "lehenga" is a contextual gender signal that fires correctly even in a code-mixed sentence, because it's matched as a garment word, not a relational phrase.

text: Buying this for my girlfriend but I also want some men's jeans for myself, budget 8000
notes: Both male and female signals present in the same sentence. Correct behavior is to refuse to guess and stay unknown, not silently pick one — this is the invariant under test, not a bug.

text: Need a complete wedding lehenga set with jewellery under 2000 rupees
notes: A realistically impossible budget for the requested item — good input for Script C-style honesty handling downstream.

text: Starting my first job next week. Need 3 formal shirts, 2 formal trousers, 1 blazer. Budget 12000. Prefer navy and white.
knownLimitation: "trousers" and "blazer" are not in the recognized garment-type list, so they're silently dropped rather than extracted. Worth adding before a first-job demo script.
notes: Shirt, budget, colors, and preference-mode all extract correctly; only the two unmapped garment words are missed.

text: Monsoon essentials for Delhi. Need 2 raincoats and 1 pair of waterproof shoes. Budget 3500.
knownLimitation: "raincoat" is not a recognized garment type. The stated quantity for shoes ("1 pair") also isn't adjacent to the word "shoes" in the text, so quantity defaults to 1 rather than being read from "pair" — coincidentally correct here, not by design.

text: Building a gym wardrobe. Need 4 tshirts, 3 joggers, 2 shorts. Budget 6000. Black and grey only, easy wash.
knownLimitation: "shorts" is not a recognized garment type and is silently dropped.

text: Diwali outfit for my dad. Need 1 kurta and 1 pair of mojaris. Budget 5000.
knownLimitation: "mojaris" (traditional footwear) is not a recognized garment type.
notes: Contrast case to hinglish_brother_kurta: the SAME relational phrase ("for my dad") correctly resolves to Men when written in English, confirming the gap is code-mixed language, not the relational pattern itself.

text: asdkjasnd kjasnd askjdn aksjdn
notes: Nonsense input. The parser correctly extracts nothing rather than inventing a plan; a separate content-sufficiency check in the /agent/plan route (not exercised by this harness) catches this class of input before it ever reaches the parser in production.

text: I need a wardrobe but I'm not sure about my budget yet
notes: The word "budget" is present but no digits follow it — correctly returns null rather than a fabricated number.

text: College wardrobe under 15000, need tees and jeans, black or navy
knownLimitation: "black or navy" (either) and "black and navy" (both) currently produce the same strict colour set — the parser can't distinguish an either/or preference from a combined strict palette.

text: Mujhe teen jeans chahiye aur ek jacket bhi, budget pandrah hazaar rupees
knownLimitation: Quantities and budget spelled as words in Hindi ("teen" = 3, "pandrah hazaar" = 15,000) are not parsed — only numerals are recognized. Both quantities default to 1 and budget is missed entirely. This is the single highest-value fix for a Hindi-heavy demo audience.

text: Need 2 sarees and 1 blouse for a family function, women's wear, budget 10000, prefer maroon and gold
knownLimitation: "blouse" is not a recognized garment type.

text: Winter wardrobe for Shimla trip. Need 2 jackets and 1 hoodie. Budget 7000. Dark colours only.
knownLimitation: "Dark colours only" names no specific recognized colour word ("dark" isn't in the colour vocabulary), so colorMode falls back to "preference" even though the user clearly meant a strict constraint. A stated colour is required for strict mode to engage at all.
notes: This is the most important known gap in the whole set — a user can say "only" and still not get strict enforcement if they never name a literal colour.

text: Just need sneakers, 2 pairs, under 3000, any color
knownLimitation: The quantity "2 pairs" is stated after the noun, not immediately before it, so it isn't picked up — quantity defaults to 1 instead of 2.

text: Budget is 5000 but honestly I can stretch to 8000 if needed, need 2 shirts
notes: By design, the extractor takes the first stated figure (the stated budget) over a later stretch-goal mention — the stretch amount is a downstream negotiation input, not the primary budget.

text: Need matching outfits for me and my girlfriend, 1 dress for her and 1 shirt for me, budget 6000
notes: By design, gender is a single plan-level field, never per item (CLAUDE.md Section 18) — "girlfriend" is the only relational signal present, so the whole plan resolves to Women even though a shirt for a man was also requested. This is the correct, intended behavior for the current architecture, not a bug; a genuinely mixed-gender cart needs two separate goals.

text: 10 complete formal outfits for office, budget 5000 total
notes: Another impossible-budget honesty case, phrased differently from Script C — no literal garment word, so item extraction is correctly empty here.

text: Festive wear for the whole family, need 3 kurtas, budget 9000, prefer gold and maroon tones
notes: "Whole family" and "kurtas" alone don't imply a single gender — correctly stays unknown.

text: Office ke liye formal shirts chahiye, 3 chahiye, budget fifteen thousand
knownLimitation: "fifteen thousand" spelled in English words is not parsed as a number, and the quantity "3" is stated separately from "shirts" so it isn't captured either — same class of gap as hinglish_word_numbers, this time in English.

text: Minimalist capsule wardrobe. 2 white shirts, 2 black jeans, 1 grey hoodie. Budget 11000.
notes: No explicit "only"/"prefer" word, so colorMode defaults to strict since colours were named — a deliberately conservative default (safer to over-constrain colour than under-constrain it).

text: I only want to spend 4000 on 2 tshirts, nothing fancy
knownLimitation: "spend 4000" has neither a currency symbol (Rs/₹) nor one of the recognized lead-in keywords (budget/around/under/for/within) directly adjacent to the number, so it's missed entirely despite being an unambiguous budget statement to a human reader. This is the most important budget-extraction gap in the set.

text: Something comfortable for rainy days in Mumbai, 2 hoodies and 1 pair of joggers, budget 4500, olive or black
Denim reset — 3 jeans, all dark wash, budget around 9000, no ripped styles
notes: "Dark wash" is a denim finish, not a recognized colour word, so colours correctly stay empty rather than guessing a hue.

text: URGENT!!! NEED 2 BLACK HOODIES BUDGET 3000 ASAP!!!
notes: Confirms case-insensitivity holds under all-caps, exclamation-heavy input.

text: I need 1 saree for my cousin's sangeet and 2 kurtas for daytime functions, total budget 18000, gold and pink shades
notes: "Total budget" is correctly read past the word "total" to the keyword+number pattern.
